title: Aurelius, Meditations §aur_06_xliii: When thou wilt comfort and cheer thyself, call to mind the several gifts and ...
body: When thou wilt comfort and cheer thyself, call to mind the several gifts and virtues of them, whom thou dost daily converse with; as for example, the industry of the one; the modesty of another; the liberality of a third; of another some other thing. For nothing can so much rejoice thee, as the resemblances and parallels of several virtues, visible and eminent in the dispositions of those who live with thee; especially when, all at once, as near as may be, they represent themselves unto thee. And therefore thou must have them always in a readiness.
author: engine

witnesses.label: Vaticanus Graecus 1950 — earliest complete Greek MS
Dalfen, Marci Aurelii Antonini Ad Se Ipsum Libri XII (Teubner 1987)
George Long translation (1862) — public domain English
